---
title: "LRPITT09: ITT analysis of phonetic spelling (SPPHON) (P)"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_convergence.qmd >}}

## Overview

**In plain terms.** Did the children offered the intervention spell more words phonetically than those who were not — and how large, and how certain, is that difference? Because group assignment was randomised, this is a genuine causal (intention-to-treat) effect of *being offered* the programme, not just an association.

LRPITT09 is the intention-to-treat (ITT) analysis for phonetic spelling
(`spphon`). The estimand is the total effect of randomised group assignment $G$ on
the outcome, using the RCT phase only (time 1 $\rightarrow$ time 2). Phonetic
spelling is heavily floored at t2 (~78% of post-scores at zero), so it takes the
pre-specified **floor-rule branch** (issue #119).

::: {.callout-important title="Floored outcome — the binary off-floor estimand is PRIMARY"}

An outcome with $\geq 40\%$ of its post-scores at zero at t2 (computed **arm-blind**
and fixed **before** fitting) is leveraged, on the graded scale, by a handful of
dispersed tail values rather than by the arm contrast. Under the floor rule this
model therefore:

- drops the degenerate own baseline and uses an **age-only** predictor;
- reports the **binary "off-floor at t2"** effect, $\Pr(\text{post} > 0)$, as the
  **PRIMARY** estimand — this is where the randomised signal verifiably lives; and
- retains the graded Beta-Binomial $\tau$ only as a flagged, **detection-limited
  SECONDARY**, read alongside the per-arm mover counts and never on its own.

:::

::: {.callout-note title="Identification and sign convention"}

Under the locked causal DAG, $G$ is a randomised root, so the ITT effect $\tau$ is
identified by the **empty adjustment set**; linear age is a precision term only.
**Sign convention:** $G = 1$ is the intervention arm, so a **positive** $\tau$ means
the intervention raises the outcome — here, raises the probability of coming off
the floor.

:::

## Model

PRIMARY (binary off-floor), with $\eta_i = \alpha + \tau\,G_i + \gamma_A\,A_{\text{std},i}$:

$$
[\,P_{\text{post},i} > 0\,] \sim \text{Bernoulli}\big(\sigma(\eta_i)\big).
$$

SECONDARY (graded, detection-limited): the *same* age-only $\eta_i$ with a
Beta-Binomial likelihood on the count, $P_{\text{post},i} \sim \text{BetaBinomial}(N, \mu_i\kappa, (1-\mu_i)\kappa)$, $N = 92$.

![Model diagram (primary)](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_diagnostics.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_results_floored.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
